client.name: Helix Cloud
client.businessModel: Subscription data observability platform sold annually to Mid-Market and Enterprise data teams

glossary.definition: Annual Recurring Revenue — annualized value of active subscriptions
glossary.whyItMatters: Primary top-line metric used to plan and measure the business

glossary.definition: New ARR + expansion ARR minus churn and contraction in a period
glossary.whyItMatters: Headline quarterly KPI; the Q2 2026 miss is measured here

glossary.definition: ARR lost from canceled subscriptions, before any expansion
glossary.whyItMatters: Mid-Market gross churn jumped from 6.8% to 11.2% in Q2

glossary.definition: Share of customers buying a specific add-on alongside the core product
glossary.whyItMatters: Helix Lineage hit 18% attach in Q2 against a 35% plan

glossary.definition: Accounts at 50 to 1,000 employees with ACV between $30,000 and $80,000
glossary.whyItMatters: Where the Q1 price hike is hitting renewals

glossary.definition: Accounts at 1,000+ employees with ACV between $80,000 and $500,000
glossary.whyItMatters: Where Lineage expansion was supposed to drive growth

glossary.definition: Ratio of open pipeline to remaining quota for the period
glossary.whyItMatters: Held at 3.4x in Q2, above the 3x target, so pipeline is not the issue

glossary.definition: Honoring an existing price for the remainder of a customer contract before applying a new list price
glossary.whyItMatters: Helix Cloud grandfathered Mid-Market customers but the uplift hit at Q2 renewals

systems.name: BigQuery (helix-cloud-prod)
systems.roleInBusiness: Central analytics warehouse with revenue, customer success, and product usage datasets
knownQuirks: Subscription rows can update late on day-of-renewal due to upstream Salesforce sync lag

systems.name: Salesforce
systems.roleInBusiness: System of record for accounts, opportunities, and renewals
knownQuirks: Stage progression was audited clean in July 2026, despite a hypothesis that it was broken

systems.name: Stripe
systems.roleInBusiness: Billing and invoicing for Mid-Market subscriptions
knownQuirks: Annual upfront invoicing means downgrades show up at renewal, not within-quarter

systems.name: Gainsight
systems.roleInBusiness: Customer success and health score management
knownQuirks: Health score weighting last retuned in March 2026, so trendlines reset around that date

regulations.name: SOC 2 Type II
regulations.summary: Annual third-party audit of security, availability, and confidentiality controls; Helix Cloud holds Type II

regulations.name: GDPR
regulations.summary: EU data protection rules apply to EU customer telemetry processed by Helix Cloud

world.clientProfile:
Helix Cloud is a Series C B2B SaaS company that provides observability for the modern data stack: logs, traces, metrics, and lineage for data pipelines and data warehouses. Headquartered in San Francisco with an engineering hub in Bangalore, it serves about 620 paying accounts, ended Q1 2026 at $58.2M ARR, and reached $65.8M ARR by end of Q2 2026 on $7.6M of net new ARR against a $9.5M target.

The company sells three products: Helix Core (the foundation), Helix Alerts (anomaly detection add-on), and Helix Lineage (column-level lineage, GA in Q4 2025). On January 15, 2026 Helix Cloud raised Mid-Market list prices by 18% on renewal. CEO Lindsey Park has asked the RevOps Analytics team for a definitive diagnosis of the Q2 miss ahead of the board readout on July 15, 2026.

personas.name: Sam Iyer
personas.backstory: Sam runs RevOps Analytics and owns the BigQuery warehouse. He hired the learner two weeks ago and is acting as their on-the-ground manager for this diagnosis. He has the cleanest map of the data layer at Helix Cloud.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Sam Iyer, Director of RevOps at Helix Cloud and the learner's manager. You are calm, helpful, and patient. You own the data warehouse and can translate what columns mean. You write like a Slack user: short messages, one thought per line, lowercase often, no em dashes, no paragraphs. You do not volunteer the full diagnosis; you point at where to look and verify hypotheses when asked. If asked about something outside your remit (marketing strategy, product roadmap), redirect to the right colleague.

privateKnowledge.value: Two key tables for this case: revenue.accounts (one row per account) and revenue.subscriptions (one row per active subscription line for Core, Alerts, or Lineage). revenue.events has billing events but you usually start with the first two.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask where the data lives or which tables to query first

privateKnowledge.value: On 2026-01-15 we raised Mid-Market list prices 18% on renewal. Existing customers were grandfathered on their current contract; the uplift hits at next renewal. Q2 was the first quarter where the renewal cohort caught the new price.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about the Q1 price change or grandfathering

privateKnowledge.value: We already audited Salesforce stage progression in early July. The data is clean. Don't waste time chasing that.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask whether Salesforce stage data is reliable

personas.openingLine:
hey welcome
so, q2 net new came in at $7.6m vs $9.5m plan
lindsey wants a clean diagnosis for the board on july 15
start with revenue.accounts and revenue.subscriptions in bigquery
ping me if you get stuck

personas.name: Maya Chen
personas.backstory: Maya runs the revenue org. She is pragmatic and deeply numeric and slightly defensive about sales execution after a tough quarter. She quotes ratios fluently and pushes back if she thinks a hypothesis is sloppy.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Maya Chen, VP Revenue at Helix Cloud. You are pragmatic, deeply numeric, and a bit defensive about sales execution. You speak in Slack style: short, casual, one thought per line, no em dashes, no paragraphs, occasional lowercase for emphasis. You quote ratios fluently. You do not hand over the answer; you give pointed facts when asked the right question. If asked about pricing rationale, you know we raised Mid-Market list 18% on Jan 15, 2026. If asked about pipeline coverage, you will defend it (3.4x, above 3x target). If asked about enterprise downgrades, you will name the three accounts.

privateKnowledge.value: Q2 pipeline coverage held at 3.4x, above the 3x target. Marketing pipeline did not collapse. Don't blame demand gen, the data won't support it.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about pipeline coverage or whether marketing dried up

privateKnowledge.value: Three strategic Enterprise accounts restructured to lower tiers in Q2: Northwind Logistics, Verity Insurance, and Atlas Manufacturing. Combined impact was roughly $190K of the miss.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about specific enterprise account downgrades

privateKnowledge.value: Mid-Market new logo sales cycle stretched from 8 weeks to 11 weeks in Q2. New logos were down 22% year over year. Not a collapse, but it slowed.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask how Mid-Market new business is trending

personas.openingLine:
ok i have 15 min
shoot
but please don't tell me marketing pipeline dried up
coverage was 3.4x. it's not that

personas.name: Jordan Park
personas.backstory: Jordan runs CS and owns retention, expansion, and the QBR motion. Warm but direct. Watches health scores closely and is candid about what is happening in the Mid-Market install base after the price change.
personas.systemPrompt: You are Jordan Park, VP Customer Success at Helix Cloud. You are warm but direct. You own retention, expansion, and the QBR motion. You write like a Slack user: short messages, one thought per line, no em dashes, no paragraphs, occasional 'tbh' and 'yeah'. You will share the churn pattern you are seeing if asked. You know that Mid-Market gross churn jumped from 6.8% to 11.2% in Q2 and most of it traces back to the January price hike landing at renewal. You are also tracking Lineage attach disappointing in the install base.

privateKnowledge.value: Mid-Market gross churn was 6.8% in Q1 and 11.2% in Q2. The spike maps almost exactly to accounts whose renewal date fell after the January 15, 2026 price uplift took effect.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about Mid-Market churn or retention trends in Q2

privateKnowledge.value: Helix Lineage attach in the install base came in at 18% in Q2 against a 35% plan. The motion is sales-led today; CS hasn't been driving it. That is fixable but it costs us this quarter.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask about Helix Lineage attach or expansion motion

privateKnowledge.value: Yes there was one P1 in Q2. 41 minutes. No revenue impact, no churn tied to it. Don't make it a thing.
privateKnowledge.disclosureHint: Ask whether the Q2 outage caused churn

personas.openingLine:
hi! glad you're digging in
tbh the q2 churn pattern is pretty clear from where i sit
ask me about renewals and i'll show you
also, lineage attach is a story too

wikiPages.title: Helix Cloud Revenue Model Overview
wikiPages.body:
# Helix Cloud Revenue Model Overview

Helix Cloud sells annual subscriptions to data and platform teams. Revenue is measured primarily in **Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR)** and the quarterly headline number is **Net New ARR**.

## Headline numbers

| Metric | Q1 2026 | Q2 2026 |
|---|---|---|
| Ending ARR | $58.2M | $65.8M |
| Net New ARR | n/a | $7.6M (target $9.5M) |
| Paying accounts | ~600 | ~620 |

Net new ARR = new ARR + expansion ARR minus churn and contraction.

## Products

- [Helix Core](wiki://products-and-pricing) — flagship, every paying account has it
- Helix Alerts — anomaly detection add-on, ~62% attach
- Helix Lineage — column-level lineage, GA Q4 2025, 18% attach in Q2 against a 35% plan

## The Q2 question

Why did net new ARR miss by $1.9M? See the [data layer reference](wiki://data-layer-reference) for where to look.

wikiPages.title: Products and Pricing
wikiPages.body:
# Products and Pricing

Helix Cloud sells three products. Every paying account starts with Helix Core; Alerts and Lineage are add-ons.

## Segments

- **Mid-Market** — 50 to 1,000 employees. ACV $30,000 to $80,000. Annual contracts.
- **Enterprise** — 1,000+ employees. ACV $80,000 to $500,000. Annual or multi-year, AE-led.

## The Q1 2026 price change

On **January 15, 2026** Helix Cloud raised Mid-Market list prices by **18%** on renewal. Existing customers were grandfathered for the remainder of their current contract; the uplift applies at next renewal.

This matters: Q2 2026 is the first quarter where a meaningful renewal cohort encountered the new price. Mid-Market gross churn was 6.8% in Q1 and 11.2% in Q2.

## Add-on attach rates (Q2 2026)

| Product | Attach | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Helix Alerts | 62% | 60% |
| Helix Lineage | 18% | 35% |

wikiPages.title: Data Layer Reference (BigQuery)
wikiPages.body:
# Data Layer Reference

All analytics live in BigQuery project **`helix-cloud-prod`**.

## Datasets and tables

- `revenue.accounts` — one row per customer account (segment, region, owner, status)
- `revenue.subscriptions` — one row per active subscription line (account, product, ACV, start, renewal date)
- `revenue.events` — billing events: new, expansion, downgrade, churn, contraction
- `revenue.pipeline` — Salesforce opportunities snapshot
- `customer_success.health_scores` — daily account health rollup
- `customer_success.tickets` — support tickets
- `product_usage.usage_daily` — per-account daily usage

## Starting points for the Q2 diagnosis

1. Pull Q2 churn by segment from `revenue.subscriptions` joined to `revenue.accounts`.
2. Cross-reference renewal dates against the **January 15, 2026** price-change effective date.
3. Look at Lineage attach in `revenue.subscriptions` filtered to `product = 'Helix Lineage'`.

## Known data quirks

- Subscription rows can update late on day-of-renewal due to Salesforce sync lag.
- Gainsight health score weighting was retuned in March 2026, so trendlines reset around that date.

wikiPages.title: Q2 2026 Board Readout Context
wikiPages.body:
# Q2 2026 Board Readout Context

**In-world date:** 2026-07-08

**Board readout:** 2026-07-15

CEO Lindsey Park has asked RevOps Analytics for a definitive diagnosis of the Q2 net new ARR miss.

## The fact pattern

- Net new ARR target: **$9.5M**
- Net new ARR actual: **$7.6M**
- Variance: **-$1.9M (-20.0%)**

## Hypotheses already off the table

- **"Marketing pipeline dried up."** Pipeline coverage held at 3.4x against a 3x target.
- **"There was a major outage."** One P1 in Q2, 41 minutes, no revenue impact.
- **"Salesforce stage progression broke."** RevOps audited stage data in early July; it is clean.

## What the deliverable looks like

A brief written diagnosis the CEO can take into the board readout, with a quantified driver decomposition that adds up to $1.9M and recommended actions tied to each driver.

wikiPages.title: Who to Talk To
wikiPages.body:
# Who to Talk To

Three people on Slack can help you triangulate the Q2 miss. Each has different visibility.

## Sam Iyer — Director, RevOps (your manager)

Owns the data warehouse. Translate column meanings, sanity-check queries, confirm what is and isn't already audited.

## Maya Chen — VP Revenue (CRO)

Owns the sales motion. Knows pipeline coverage, sales cycle length, and which enterprise accounts restructured this quarter. Defensive about sales execution; be precise with questions.

## Jordan Park — VP Customer Success

Owns retention and expansion. Knows the Mid-Market churn jump, the Lineage attach shortfall, and the install-base picture.

## How to ask

Keep messages short and direct. Each person knows specific things they will share when asked the right question. They will not volunteer the full diagnosis.

modules.title: Diagnose the Q2 2026 Net New ARR Miss
modules.objective: Develop a quantified, evidence-backed diagnosis of why Helix Cloud missed its Q2 2026 net new ARR target by $1.9M, ready for the July 15 board readout.

tasks.title: Diagnose the Q2 2026 Revenue Miss
tasks.brief:
# Diagnose the Q2 2026 Revenue Miss

Welcome to Helix Cloud. You just joined RevOps Analytics as a Data Analyst. Today is July 8, 2026 and the board readout is on July 15.

Q2 2026 net new ARR came in at **$7.6M** against a plan of **$9.5M**. CEO Lindsey Park wants a definitive diagnosis from this team.

## What you need to deliver

A short written diagnosis with:
- A **driver decomposition** that quantifies how much of the $1.9M miss is explained by each driver and **adds up to $1.9M**
- **Evidence** for each driver, traceable to the BigQuery warehouse (`revenue.accounts`, `revenue.subscriptions`, etc.) or to a specific stakeholder conversation
- A list of **hypotheses you ruled out** and why
- **Recommended actions** tied to each driver

## How to work

1. Start with the [revenue model overview](wiki://revenue-model-overview) and the [data layer reference](wiki://data-layer-reference).
2. Chat with Sam, Maya, and Jordan. Each holds different pieces; you have to ask the right questions.
3. Pull the numbers yourself from BigQuery. Don't trust a number you haven't reconciled.
4. Upload your diagnosis when ready.

## Tips

- The miss is roughly $1.9M. Your decomposition should sum to that, not feel close to it.
- Don't chase the obvious red herrings (pipeline collapse, the outage, Salesforce stage data). They are already off the table.
- Pay attention to the January 15, 2026 price change and which renewal cohort caught it first.

deliverable.acceptanceSummary: A written Q2 2026 revenue miss diagnosis with a quantified driver decomposition summing to $1.9M, evidence for each driver, ruled-out hypotheses, and recommended actions.

hardCriteria.description: Driver decomposition is present and the components sum to approximately $1.9M (within ±10%)
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Check that named drivers each have a dollar value and that the values sum to $1.7M to $2.1M

hardCriteria.description: Mid-Market churn from the Q1 price hike is identified as the largest driver
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Check that Mid-Market churn linked to the Jan 15, 2026 price uplift is the largest line in the decomposition

hardCriteria.description: At least 3 distinct drivers are named (Mid-Market churn, Enterprise expansion / Lineage shortfall, slower new-logo Mid-Market, or Enterprise downgrades)
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Count uniquely named drivers in the decomposition

hardCriteria.description: At least one red herring is explicitly ruled out with reasoning (pipeline collapse, the Q2 outage, or Salesforce stage data)
hardCriteria.verificationMethod: Look for a section or sentence ruling out a non-cause with evidence

scoringGuide.one: Numbers asserted without source; no reconciliation to warehouse or interviews
scoringGuide.three: Most drivers are sourced to warehouse tables or specific persona conversations
scoringGuide.five: Every driver is traced to a warehouse table or interview quote, and the decomposition reconciles cleanly to the $1.9M miss

qualitativeCriteria.description: Recommended actions are specific and tied to drivers

scoringGuide.three: Actions exist and are mapped to drivers but are broad
scoringGuide.five: Actions are specific, time-bound where appropriate, and each one is directly tied to a quantified driver

rubric.passCondition: All 4 hard criteria met AND average qualitative score >= 3

tasks.notes: First task. The learner builds the diagnosis from warehouse data plus three Slack-style stakeholder conversations.
